TSLA: Tesla to invest $250 million in battery plant outside Berlin. Tesla announced it will invest nearly $250 million to increase battery cell production capacity at its factory near Berlin. This investment aims to boost the annual production capacity at the Gruenheide site to 18 gigawatt hours from 8 GWh, with a significant increase in labor demand anticipated. The company plans for battery cells and electric vehicles to be produced at this single location starting in 2027.

TSLA Stock Rises After Piper Sandler Declares Optimus Is Free Ahead Of Musk China Visit. Tesla Inc. (TSLA) stock rose 4% after Piper Sandler analyst Alexander Potter stated that investors are essentially getting Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot business for free at current share prices, excluding its value from the firm's $400 per share target. The stock also benefited from news of Elon Musk's impending visit to China with President Donald Trump, aiming to strengthen business ties. Potter reiterated a $500 price target for Tesla, with $100 attributed to Optimus and other unanalyzed ventures.

Apple, Boeing, Citi, Tesla, Meta executives to join Trump's China trip. Over a dozen CEOs and top executives from major U.S. companies, including Apple, Boeing, Citigroup, Tesla, and Meta, are expected to accompany President Donald Trump on his trip to China this week. The delegation highlights significant corporate interest in U.S.-China relations, with prominent figures like Tim Cook, Larry Fink, and Elon Musk participating.

Musk, Cook set to join Trump for Xi Summit, White House says. The White House is inviting prominent business leaders, including Elon Musk of Tesla, Tim Cook of Apple, and executives from Goldman Sachs, Blackstone, and BlackRock, to accompany President Donald Trump on his trip to China for a summit with Xi Jinping. The delegation aims to secure business deals and purchase agreements, focusing primarily on financial, technology, aerospace, and agricultural firms. Notably absent from the delegation is Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang, which could signal a setback for the company's AI chip exports to China.

Tesla $400 core value leaves Optimus & AI upside fully unpriced: Piper analyst view. Piper Sandler analysts suggest that Tesla's current stock price of $400 per share accurately reflects the value of its Electric Vehicle and energy businesses. This valuation implies that potentially lucrative ventures like Optimus, robotaxis, and artificial intelligence present a significant uncatalyzed upside for investors, not yet factored into the stock price.

Tesla heads off trial in Gigafactory Texas assault case tied to mass shooting. Tesla has successfully avoided a trial in an assault case linked to a former employee suspected in a mass shooting in Austin. The company was granted a motion for arbitration in the lawsuit brought by Lillian Brady, who alleged she was assaulted at the Gigafactory by Ndiaga Diagne, the same individual later involved in the fatal shooting incident. Brady accused Tesla of failing to provide a safe work environment and not adequately vetting employees.
TGT: Target Dishes on Comprehensive Store Remodels. Target is accelerating its store remodeling efforts, investing approximately $5 billion this year in over 130 remodels and 30 new stores to enhance the shopping experience. These transformations focus on creating more intuitive layouts, expanding assortments, and improving convenience for services like Order Pickup and Drive Up. The comprehensive upgrades aim to strengthen Target's physical stores as a key differentiator and support its long-term growth and fulfillment services.

Target in biggest store remodel program in a decade — here are the details. Target is embarking on its most extensive store remodeling program in over a decade, updating more than 130 locations this year, opening 30 new stores, and upgrading supply chain technology. The remodels will feature expanded grocery selections, modern aesthetics, improved order fulfillment areas, and enhanced guest amenities. These efforts aim to create a more intuitive, inspiring, and convenient shopping experience for customers while also reducing energy costs and strengthening the stores' role in fulfillment.
SBUX: Stifel Reaffirms Bullish View on Starbucks Corporation (SBUX). Stifel analyst Chris O’Cull has raised the price recommendation for Starbucks Corporation (SBUX) to $117 from $115, maintaining a Buy rating. This follows Starbucks' Q2 2026 earnings call where the company reported revenue and earnings growth for the first time in over two years, prompting a raise in fiscal 2026 guidance. North America was noted as Starbucks’ best-performing segment, and the company plans to introduce new customer and employee initiatives.

Starbucks lays off 61 more corporate workers in Seattle. Starbucks has laid off an additional 61 corporate workers in its Seattle headquarters, specifically within the technology department. This follows previous layoffs affecting both corporate and retail employees, and occurs amidst the company's plan to relocate or hire 2,000 workers for a new Nashville office. Despite these changes, Seattle will remain Starbucks' global headquarters, and the company recently reported strong Q2 growth with revenue reaching $9.5 billion.

5 Consumer Discretionary Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session. This article identifies five consumer discretionary stocks—QuantumScape (QS), Sweetgreen (SG), Amazon (AMZN), Pinduoduo (PDD), and Starbucks (SBUX)—that recently exhibited significant "whale" options trading activity. Benzinga's scanner detected unusual call option movements for these companies, indicating potential trading opportunities based on bullish, bearish, or neutral sentiment among large investors. The article details specific option contracts, strike prices, expiration dates, and trade types for each stock.

Starbucks lays off 61 Seattle tech employees. Starbucks has laid off 61 technology employees in Seattle as part of a significant turnaround effort led by CEO Brian Niccol. This move affects a portion of the approximately 9,500 Starbucks employees in the Seattle area. The article highlights this development amidst other business news and upcoming events in the Puget Sound region.

Starbucks tech layoffs hit Seattle workers. Starbucks is laying off 61 tech workers in Seattle as part of a reorganization within its technology department at the Starbucks Support Center. This move follows the company's internal announcement last month and is part of a broader "Back to Starbucks" plan to refocus on the coffeehouse experience. The layoffs, primarily affecting project managers, scrum masters, systems administrators, and technical product managers, are scheduled to begin in June, with affected employees separated by August.

Starbucks to cut 61 technology jobs in Seattle. Starbucks announced it will lay off 61 technology workers at its Seattle headquarters as part of a broader turnaround strategy to enhance customer experience and drive revenue growth. These job cuts are separate from a previous plan to relocate some technology roles to Nashville and follow earlier restructuring efforts including store closures and corporate layoffs. The layoffs are scheduled to begin in June and are not due to relocation.

Starbucks cuts 61 technology jobs in Seattle. Starbucks has cut 61 corporate technology jobs at its Seattle headquarters, as disclosed in a WARN filing. This move is part of the company's turnaround strategy focused on improving customer satisfaction and sales metrics, with the layoffs not related to a planned relocation of some tech jobs to Nashville. The company had previously laid off around 1,100 corporate workers and closed underperforming stores as part of its restructuring efforts.
DIS: WEBTOON's U.S. headquarters posts more than 10 billion won in operating loss in Q1. WEBTOON Entertainment Inc., Naver Webtoon's U.S. headquarters, reported an operating loss of over 10 billion won in Q1, although this marks a significant reduction from the prior year. Revenue saw a slight dip, but the company noted increased monthly paying users in Korea and globally. Strategic content diversification, including collaborations with Disney and adaptations of Wattpad novels, is key to their turnaround strategy.
NKE: U.S. shares higher at close of trade; Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.19%. U.S. equities closed higher on Monday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising 0.19%, the S&P 500 climbing 0.19%, and the NASDAQ Composite adding 0.10%. Gains were primarily driven by the Oil & Gas, Basic Materials, and Industrials sectors. Caterpillar, Honeywell International, and Cisco Systems were among the top performers on the Dow, while Nike, Walt Disney Company, and International Business Machines saw declines.
